reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #tsc-proves-rename-completeness-grep-proves-the-rest: a pure identifier rename (MAGIC_12_KEYS -> MAGIC_COMBO_KEYS) is exactly what tsc fully validates FOR THE CODE -- any missed or typo'd reference fails the build, so the green typecheck IS the code-completeness proof. But tsc is blind to two non-type surfaces: (a) string-literal / dynamic access (obj['MAGIC_12_KEYS'], a test asserting on the NAME, a serialized key) which type-checks fine while silently breaking, and (b) live-doc staleness. So the CHECK grepped the LITERAL OLD name across the whole repo: 0 dynamic/string hits in src, 11 doc hits -- each classified current-state (would-be-false -> fix) vs dated-history (handoff/archive/reflexion/session-state task label -> LEAVE, editing is revisionist). All 11 were history. LESSON: for an identifier rename let tsc carry code-completeness, but STILL grep the literal old name across src+docs for the two things tsc cannot see -- dynamic/string access and present-tense doc claims -- and split doc hits current-vs-dated before touching any.

P1 #prove-a-test-loops-contribution-before-rescaling-it: session15's solo-win loop ran PHASE_1_WIN_SCORE/SCORE_ANCHOR (=630) iterations and LOOKED load-bearing. Before pinning it to a fixed 12, I verified the loop is INERT to the assertions: the win is driven by the direct w.scoreProgress=PHASE_1_WIN_SCORE set on the very next line, and the CHECK confirmed via gameState.ts:59 that WIN gates ONLY on floor(scoreProgress)>=threshold while placePrimitive never writes scoreProgress/scoreByPlayer -- so 12 vs 630 changes nothing the test asserts (the sibling 1v1 test wins with ZERO placements). LESSON: before rescaling/removing a test loop, trace whether any ASSERTION depends on its iteration count; a loop that only mutates state the assertions never read is safe to pin to a small intentional constant -- and decoupling it from a balance constant structurally prevents a future rebalance from silently changing the dispatch count (closes the S91 #a-relative-test-still-breaks-on-a-fixed-iteration-budget lesson at this site).
